I find it helpful to read a saxpy and GEMM kernel for a new accelerator like this - do they have an example?
Curious if the architecture is similar to what is called “systolic” as in the Anton series of supercomputers: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anton_(computer)
If there really is enough market demand for this kind of processor, it seems like someone like NEC who still makes vector processors would be better poised than a startup rolling RISC-V

The other company I can think of focusing on F64 is Fujitsu with its A64FX processor.  This is an ARM64 with really meaty SIMD to get 3TFLOP of FP64.I guess it it hard to compare chip for chip but the question is, if you are building a supercomputer (and we ignore pressure to buy sovereign) then which is better bang for the buck on representative workloads?
Sounds like an idea that would really benefit from a JIT-like approach to basically every software.

The riscv was the "one more thing" aspect of the "reveal".The main product/architecture discussed  has nothing to do with vector processors or riscv.It's a new, fundamentally different data-flow processor.Hopefully we will improve in explaining what we do and why people may want to care.
